title: Subtype Entities
text: Subtype Entities Subtype Entities (also referred to as subtypes) are specializations of modeled entities. A Subtype Entity is the equivalent of an alias for a relational table and does not exist as a physical table in its own right. Fields and relationships defined for the supertype entity (the entity specialized by the subtype) are reproduced in the subtype. Subtype Entities can be used in circumstances when you need to: Define multiple relationships between two entities. Define a one-to-many relationship where both the one entity and the many entity are the same entity—for example, a manager is an employee, and also has a relationship with multiple other employees. Create sub-definitions or roles in an entity—for example, country can be country of birth and country of residence. Define a set of constraints in an entity or define subsets of data. The referential integrity for subtypes is the same as their supertypes. Thus, if you define a relationship between two entities, the relationship is inherited by the subtypes of the two entities. This can be overridden by defining relationships for the subtypes themselves. For example, if you define two relationships, one for the subtype and one for the supertype, both use the same Many entity. The relationship that is used depends on whether you use the subtype or the supertype in the component. At runtime, Uniface tries to match the primary key in the one entity to a field in the many entity. This can make it appear as though a subtype’s relationship is being inherited by its supertype.
